Upstate NY pest guide

Know Your Enemy: Common Pests in Upstate NY

Upstate New York’s unique climate, forests, farms, older homes, and dramatic seasons make it prime territory for a wide variety of pests. From spring ant invasions to winter mice, knowing what you’re seeing is the first step to protecting your home.

Request a Free Inspection Browse Pest Profiles

Common risk zones

Homes, attics, basements, barns, sheds

Best defense

Fast identification + targeted treatment

Biggest seasonal spike

Fall invasions & winter overwintering

Local reality

Rural, suburban, and wooded properties all face pressure

Seasonal swings

Cold winters push rodents, cluster flies, and overwintering insects indoors. Warm months bring ants, ticks, stinging insects, and spiders.

Forests and fields

Wooded edges and rural landscapes create ideal habitat for mice, ticks, raccoons, skunks, and termites near structures.

Older homes

Cracks, voids, crawlspaces, and aging wood give pests easy access and hidden places to nest, feed, and spread.

Detailed Pest Profiles for Upstate New York

Use these field notes to identify the pest, understand the risk, and decide when it’s time to call in professional help.

🐜 Carpenter Ants

What it looks like: Large black ants, often 1/4" to 1/2" long.
Where found: Damp or softened wood, wall voids, decks, window trim, and structural framing.
Why it’s a problem: They tunnel through wood and can weaken structures over time.
Signs of infestation: Sawdust-like frass, rustling in walls, winged ants, and trails near moisture.
Peak season in Upstate NY: Spring and summer.

Get Help With This Pest

🐭 White-Footed Mouse

What it looks like: Small brown mouse with white feet and underbelly.
Where found: Rural properties, sheds, garages, brush piles, and wooded edge homes.
Why it’s a problem: Contaminates food, damages insulation and wiring, and can support tick spread associated with Lyme risk.
Signs of infestation: Droppings, gnaw marks, scratching sounds, nest material, and chewed packaging.
Peak season in Upstate NY: Fall when seeking warmth.

Get Help With This Pest

🦟 Deer Tick / Black-Legged Tick

What it looks like: Tiny dark tick; adults and nymphs are hard to spot.
Where found: Forests, brush, tall grass, landscaping edges, and suburban backyards.
Why it’s a problem: Major Lyme disease vector in Upstate New York.
Signs of infestation: Ticks on pets, clothing, or skin; frequent bites after outdoor activity.
Peak season in Upstate NY: Spring through fall.

Get Help With This Pest

🪲 Brown Marmorated Stink Bug

What it looks like: Shield-shaped brown bug with mottled markings and a stink defense.
Where found: Sunlit walls, windows, attics, and indoor hiding spots in the fall.
Why it’s a problem: Overwinters indoors and becomes a nuisance in large numbers.
Signs of infestation: Clustered bugs on sunny walls, buzzing in windows, and odor when disturbed.
Peak season in Upstate NY: Fall.

Get Help With This Pest

🐝 Yellowjackets & Bald-Faced Hornets

What it looks like: Yellow-and-black wasps; hornets are black-and-white with large paper nests.
Where found: Ground nests, wall voids, eaves, trees, and shrubs.
Why it’s a problem: Aggressive defenders that can sting repeatedly and threaten families, pets, and workers.
Signs of infestation: Heavy insect traffic near a nest, buzzing in walls, visible paper nest, or wasps around food.
Peak season in Upstate NY: Late summer.

Get Help With This Pest

🕷️ Wolf Spider

What it looks like: Large, fast-moving spider with a hairy body and strong legs.
Where found: Basements, garages, sheds, crawlspaces, and occasionally living areas in fall.
Why it’s a problem: Alarming in appearance, though mostly harmless; finding many indoors may signal prey insects nearby.
Signs of infestation: Occasional large spiders at floor level, especially near doors, windows, and stored items.
Peak season in Upstate NY: Fall.

Get Help With This Pest

🪲 Spotted Lanternfly

What it looks like: Distinct spotted wings, red underwings, and black-tipped body.
Where found: Trees, vines, outdoor structures, and high-traffic landscape areas.
Why it’s a problem: Invasive species that damages plants, trees, and outdoor spaces while spreading quickly.
Signs of infestation: Swarms on trees, sticky honeydew, sooty mold, and egg masses on surfaces.
Peak season in Upstate NY: Summer into fall.

Get Help With This Pest

🪳 German Cockroach

What it looks like: Small tan-brown roach with two dark stripes behind the head.
Where found: Kitchens, restaurants, appliance gaps, cabinets, and warm moist voids.
Why it’s a problem: Breeds rapidly, contaminates surfaces, and spreads in multi-unit and food-service environments.
Signs of infestation: Droppings, egg cases, greasy smears, and live roaches at night.
Peak season in Upstate NY: Year-round indoors.

Get Help With This Pest

🦝 Raccoon

What it looks like: Medium-sized mammal with a mask-like face and ringed tail.
Where found: Neighborhoods, attics, chimneys, sheds, garbage areas, and rooflines.
Why it’s a problem: Tearing into trash, damaging roofs, and nesting in attics can cause costly repairs and contamination.
Signs of infestation: Overturned trash, roof damage, scratching overhead, and raccoon droppings.
Peak season in Upstate NY: Spring through fall.

Get Help With This Pest

🐀 Norway Rat

What it looks like: Large, heavy-bodied brown or gray rat with a blunt nose and scaly tail.
Where found: Near waterways, sewer lines, dumpsters, basements, and foundation gaps.
Why it’s a problem: Major structural and health pest that can chew wiring, contaminate food, and multiply quickly.
Signs of infestation: Large droppings, burrows, greasy rub marks, gnawing, and nighttime activity.
Peak season in Upstate NY: Fall and winter indoors.

Get Help With This Pest

🦟 Cluster Fly

What it looks like: Slow-moving dark fly that gathers in large numbers on sunny walls and windows.
Where found: Attics, wall voids, rafters, and upper levels of rural homes and barns.
Why it’s a problem: They overwinter in walls, then emerge in huge groups on warm days.
Signs of infestation: Lazy flies at windows, buzzing in attics, and masses appearing indoors in winter.
Peak season in Upstate NY: Fall into winter.

Get Help With This Pest

🦨 Skunk

What it looks like: Black fur with bold white stripes and a bushy tail.
Where found: Under porches, decks, sheds, and burrows near foundations.
Why it’s a problem: Digs under structures, smells up properties, and can carry rabies in the wild.
Signs of infestation: Cone-shaped lawn holes, odor, tracks, and burrow openings beneath buildings.
Peak season in Upstate NY: Spring and summer.

Get Help With This Pest

🐜 Odorous House Ant

What it looks like: Tiny brown to black ants forming long trails.
Where found: Kitchens, sinks, pantries, moisture areas, and around foundations.
Why it’s a problem: Quickly establishes trails indoors and smells like coconut when crushed.
Signs of infestation: Ant lines to food or water, small gaps in baseboards, and repeated kitchen sightings.
Peak season in Upstate NY: Spring through summer.

Get Help With This Pest

🏠 Subterranean Termite

What it looks like: Small pale termite that lives underground and feeds on wood from the inside out.
Where found: Foundation edges, crawlspaces, sill plates, and hidden wood in older homes.
Why it’s a problem: Silent wood destroyer that can cause serious structural damage before it’s noticed.
Signs of infestation: Mud tubes, soft wood, blistering paint, swarmers, and hollow-sounding beams.
Peak season in Upstate NY: Spring swarms and warm wet periods.

Get Help With This Pest

Seasonal Pest Guide for Upstate NY

Spring

Watch for carpenter ants, termite swarms, ticks, skunks, and active stinging insect nests building early.

Summer

Peak season for yellowjackets, hornets, ticks, ants, lanternflies, and outdoor wildlife pressure.

Fall

The big invasion season for mice, stink bugs, cluster flies, spiders, and raccoons seeking shelter.

Winter

Rodents, cluster flies, and cockroaches remain active indoors while other pests overwinter in walls and attics.

How to Reduce Pest Pressure Around Your Property

A few smart habits can make your home far less attractive to pests common across Upstate New York.

  • Seal cracks, gaps, and utility penetrations around foundations, doors, and windows.
  • Keep firewood, leaf piles, and brush away from the home’s exterior.
  • Store food in sealed containers and clean crumbs, grease, and standing water quickly.
  • Trim back shrubs and manage moisture near basements, crawlspaces, and attics.

Need a pro’s eyes on it?

Many pests hide in wall voids, attics, and crawlspaces long before you notice damage. A professional inspection can catch the problem early.
